item.title: Journal Articles
item.description:
Peer-reviewed

  1. Welfare Cost of Inflation Revisited: the Role of Financial Innovation and Household Heterogeneity (with Césaire A. Meh, José-Vı́ctor Rı́os-Rull, and Yaz Terajima), Journal of Monetary Economics, forthcoming. [link] [Working paper] [Slides]

    Significant differences in money holding across households. Welfare cost of inflation is mostly borne by the poor and the old.

  2. Credit constraints and productivity of SMEs: Evidence from Canada (with Danny Leung), Economic Modelling, 88, 163-180, 2020. [link] [Working paper].

    The 2020 Best Paper Award, Economic Modelling, category Financial Economics.

    In 2011, about 23 percent of small and medium sized businesses in Canada were constrained by external credit to a degree. If not taking into account credit constraint, productivity estimate is upward biased by an average 4 percent.

    Government subsidy to SMEs may be ineffective in promoting growth, some financially constrained firms are unproductive.

  3. Trends in Firm Entry and New Entrepreneurship in Canada (with M. Salameh, M. Seki, and P. St-Amant), Canadian Public Policy, 43 (3), 202-220, 2017. [link]

    Since the early 1980s, the firm entry rate and the rate of new entrepreneurship have trended downward in Canada. Population aging may account for part of the decline in new entrepreneurship only since around 2000.

  4. Real GDI, productivity and the terms of trade in Canada (with Sharon Kozicki), Review of income and Wealth, 63, s1, 2017. [link]

  5. Pricing-to-market, currency invoicing and exchange rate pass-through to producer prices (with Wei Dong and Ben Tomlin), Journal of International Money and Finance, 58(C), 128-149, 2015. [link]

Other

  1. The changing pace of labour reallocation in Canada: causes and consequences (with D. Leung), Bank of Canada Review, 2009 Summer. [link]

Supplementary notes can be added here, including code, math, and images.

item.title: Working papers
item.description:
• Production Networks and the Propagation of Commodity Price Shocks (with Wei Dong), 2020. [PDF]

  • Accounting for productivity growth in a small open economy: sector-specific technology and relative prices of trade, SEF Working Paper 7/2017, VUW. [link]

  • A New Data Set of Quarterly Total Factor Productivity in the Canadian Business Sector (with S. Kozicki), Bank of Canada working paper 2015-06. [link]

  • Fixed-term and permanent employment contracts: theory and evidence (with E. Shao and P. Silos), Bank of Canada working paper 2011-21. [link]

  • Flexibility versus stability: role of temporary employment in job reallocation (with D. Leung), Bank of Canada working paper 2010-27. [link]

  • Ownership change and productivity growth in China’s manufacturing industry: 1998-2007 (with J. Liu), manuscript, 2012. [link]

  • Labour reallocation, relative prices and productivity (with D. Leung), Bank of Canada working paper 2010-2. [link]

  • A model of costly reallocation and aggregate productivity, Bank of Canada working paper, 2008. PDF. Revised, 2011. [PDF]

To be revived

  • Effects of social security policy with endogenous health and retirement, 2011.
Click the Cite button above to demo the feature to enable visitors to import publication metadata into their reference management software.
Create your slides in Markdown - click the Slides button to check out the example.

Supplementary notes can be added here, including code, math, and images.
